title: Publish flow creates a revision snapshot
description: Implement `pages.publish`, the explicit publish action that snapshots all tabs/blocks of a page into a new `revisions` row and marks the content as the current published version.
acceptanceCriteria: `pages.publish` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts `{ pageId, mensagem? }`, reads all tabs and their current `blocks` for the page, and inserts a `revisions` row with the full `PageSnapshot`
`autor_id` on the created revision is the calling user's id (from context), not client-supplied
'Publicar' button in the admin panel calls this mutation and shows success/error feedback
Autosave (TASK-32) continues to work independently and does not call this mutation

The public-facing read path (TASK-50) reads from the latest revision, not from live/draft `blocks`, once this task and TASK-50 are both complete — document this ordering dependency clearly for whoever implements TASK-50

steps.details: Add `publish: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ pageId: z.string(), mensagem: z.string().optional() })).mutation(...)` to `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/pages.ts`. Query all tabs for the page, then all blocks per tab, assemble a `PageSnapshot`, and insert into `revisions` with `autorId: ctx.user.userId`.

steps.description: Add Publicar button to the content editor UI
steps.details: In `apps/admin/src/features/editor/ContentEditor.tsx` (or a page-level toolbar wrapping all its tabs), add a 'Publicar' button calling `pages.publish.mutate({ pageId })`, with a loading state and success/error toast.

steps.description: Guard against publishing with unsaved autosave in flight
steps.details: Before publishing, ensure any pending debounced autosave (TASK-32) is flushed first, so the snapshot reflects the very latest edits rather than a stale draft.

Using Playwright: edit content across two tabs of a page, click Publicar, and confirm a new revision appears (verifiable via a direct query or the history UI once TASK-35 exists) containing both tabs' current content.

technicalNotes: This is the only place a `revisions` row is created in the MVP — do not add revision creation anywhere else (autosave, section/tab CRUD, etc.).
